violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the vegetable chopper have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Tamales, beef broth, and chicken, which was previously cooked and cooled, were not reheated for hot holding. Water in the steam table was only at 133 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025
Beef and pork, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours. Beef and pork were reheated. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pork and beef were observed cooling on the counter in the basement food prep area in foil-wrapped containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025
Single service items observed stored on shelves in the basement that are not at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025
The vegetable chopper in the basement food prep area was not in good repair, with teeth missing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the basement chest freezer have an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025
The area outside along the fence and around the rodent traps has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and dead vegetation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025

The floors especially beneath shelves in the basement storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (food chopper and dishes) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed item in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sinks in the kitchen and bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Establishment must maintain a daily sanitizer log. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded chicken. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC discarded chicken. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the dessert refrigeration unit. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. PIC added a thermometer. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Open container of chips were observed stored on a shelf next to the trashcan. Food must be stored in a clean dry location where it is not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination. PIC relocated chips and covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Splash guard needed at handwashing sink in the kitchen next to the clean storage rack. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
Clean linens were observed stored in the basement without protective covering. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Single-use items (old tomato cardboard box) was observed storing peeled onions. PIC relocated onions to bowl. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sinks in the kitchen and bar. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The walls in the basement food prep area are constructed of drywall. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025
Ceiling tiles in the basement are bulging and stained with previous water damage, The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Spanish Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bar area was lacking paper towels. PIC replaced paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
A sponge was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed sponge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Open containers of food product were observed stacked on top of each other in the prep unit. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Pico de gallo was observed in an over filled container, with excess touching unclean prep unit located in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives, pizza paddle, vegetable peeler, can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC put in 3 bay to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sinks in the kitchen and bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be ( ) ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Meat tamales were observed held at 118 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the dessert and prep unit refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Avacados were not washed before being cut. Raw fruits and vegetables must be thoroughly washed in water to remove soil and other contaminants before they are cut. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients.Some scoops observed stored with the handles in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC removed bowls. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The pizza paddle is stored on an unclean surface (top of the pizza oven) between uses.During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC relocated paddle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Clean linens were observed stored in the basement without protective covering. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Single-use items (old food containers) are being used for food preparation and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Single-use items (old food containers) are being used for food preparation and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
The ice machine was not in good repair, with the gasket detached and part of it missing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025

Cardboard is used to line shelves in the basement storage area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Food observed stored in non-food grade containers. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-food chopper and containers in the basement,-cold drawers/gaskets in the kitchen,-prep units in kitchen area. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The walls in the basement food prep area are constructed of drywall. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025
Ceiling tiles in the basement are bulging and stained with previous water damage. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025
The coving/edging at the bottom of the basement food prep area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in vent have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

The used oil tank outside is located on soil. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

Wood is used in the construction of the prep counters in the basement food prep area, which is now in poor repair with gaps along edge pieces. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
Dumpster area has an accumulation of construction materials and unused items. Dumpster area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
The ceiling in the upstairs kitchen and bar was removed. A protective barrier must be installed in food prep areas. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement food prep area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The kitchen service window is made of wood and is in poor repair, with gaps between pieces of wood, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The wall holding the mop and other cleaning implements is not made of an approved material. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
The ceiling in the basement food prep area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2025
The floor at the top of the basement stairs is in poor repair, exposing the subflooring below. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
